title: Normal traffic
description: Calls the root and health endpoints. Use this to show request rate moving without creating errors.
watch: Grafana Request Rate should rise. Prometheus request-rate PromQL should return a larger value after the next scrape.

title: 500 error burst
description: Calls the intentional /fail endpoint. Use this to show why error-rate panels and alerts matter.
watch: Grafana Error Rate should rise. Prometheus 5xx and error-percentage queries should move after the next scrape.

title: Slow request burst
description: Calls the /slow endpoint. Use this to show that success responses can still hurt users when latency rises.
watch: Grafana Latency P95 should rise. Prometheus p95 latency by namespace should return a higher value after the next scrape.

title: Mixed incident
description: Mixes normal, slow, and failing requests. Use this to practice reading multiple signals together.
watch: Request rate, 5xx error rate, and p95 latency can all move. This is the scenario closest to a real triage conversation.
